data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Let me collect the versions and create the CPM setup:

| Package | Versions | Highest |
|---|---|---|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1, 8.0.4 | **10.0.1** |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2, 1.11.4 | **1.13.2** |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | 8.0.1 | 8.0.1 |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | 1.15.0 | 1.15.0 |
| xunit | 2.9.3 | 2.9.3 |

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to:

1. Collect all packages and their versions, picking the highest version for conflicts:
   - System.Text.Json: 10.0.1 (Api, Shared, Tests) vs 8.0.4 (Worker) → 10.0.1
   - Azure.Identity: 1.13.2 (Api) vs 1.11.4 (Shared) → 1.13.2
   - Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 8.0.1 (Worker only)
   - OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0 (Worker only)
   - xunit: 2.9.3 (Tests only)

2. Create Directory.Packages.props with ManagePackageVersionsCentrally enabled and all PackageVersion entries, then remove the Version attributes from each csproj file.

    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
